_comment: Seed benchmark for correction_gate (issue #83). Cases are seeded from REAL session transcripts / documented behaviour, not synthetic scenarios (per eval-realism: Opus 4.8 writes for the inferred grader). Held-out trigger cases are checked DIFFERENTIALLY: a candidate trigger set must fire exactly the same triggers on each past input as the current baseline does — a new trigger that starts firing on one of these unrelated inputs (bloat / false-positive) is a regression. 'expected' is optional and used only as a staleness signal; leave it empty for held-out cases and rely on the differential check. Extend this file when therapy mines a new held-out input from a transcript.
_held_in_note: The motivating (held-in) case is supplied by the therapy write-path at correction time, not stored here — it is specific to the correction being proposed.

trigger.name: fuse-mount-question
trigger.input: how does the /mnt/muninn fuse mount project memories as markdown files?

trigger.name: boot-recovery-cold-start
trigger.input: cold start boot came up degraded, codeload 403 on the spokes, need add_repo

trigger.name: turso-503-cold-start
trigger.input: first recall call failed with a 503 from the proxy on cold start

trigger.name: blog-publish-reindex
trigger.input: published a whtwnd blog post, now rebuild the muninn search index

trigger.name: unrelated-calendar-chore
trigger.input: what meetings do I have on my calendar tomorrow afternoon?
trigger.source: negative case — no memory trigger should start firing on a plain calendar lookup
